- Table 3.1 of NZS 3101 assumes that members are in constant contact with water whereas Table 8 of NZS 3106 provides further information and differentiates between predominantly submerged and alternating wet and dry conditions. For alternating wet and dry conditions, NZS 3109 classifies the exposure as either B1 or B2 regardless of water hardness. Classification U is only applied to the flowing, predominantly submerged condition.
- The column bases are above the low water level and normal water levels fluctuate throughout the day based on outflows from

_Captured Friday, 18 November 2022 9:01 AM · General › General / Misc_

- Reference documents;
- CA 02 - Suspended concrete floors - maximum size of pour allowable and location of construction joints
- Each construction joint is a potential weakness in the slab and liable to be the location of a slab opening.
- The maximum slab area to be poured at one time will be dependent upon the following factors
- The importance attached to the avoidance of cracking
- The position and size of vertical members (Columns, walls,…)
- The rigidity of restraints
- The likely degree of shrinkage and the percentage of reinforcement
- Thermal and moisture movements
- The use of high cement content concrete poured in hot weather is more likely to lead to early thermal cracking.
- Progressive (consecutive) sequences of pours are preferred to alternate pours
- if overall tensions are produced due to restrained contraction, will open to form straight crack lines.
- proprietary steel mesh to form construction joints;
- bpmp2003_04 - Use of Proprietary Expanded Metal Construction Joints and Shear Keys
- Gantry check

_Captured Thursday, 19 February 2026 9:35 am · General › Concrete Design_

- ACI 213R-14 will tell you all you need to know about structural lightweight concrete properties and design. ACI 318 also defines characteristics of structural lightweight concrete. You can download ACI documents from Opusweb http://intranet/external/standards .
- ASTM STP 169D describes structural lightweight aggregate concrete as having an equilibrium density between 1440-1840kg/m3 and compressive strength >17MPa. Equilibrium density is density of hardened concrete after 90 days of air drying and is used for calculating design loads. For precast units or critical load conditions occur during construction, fresh density should be used to calculate self-load prior to equilibrium density being achieved (ACI 213R 5.3.2). ASTM STP 169D says that acceptance limits during construction should be based on fresh density.
- Thus you need to specify the required compressive strength (ultimate, and possibly at an earlier age depending on construction process), fresh density, and equilibrium density. Fresh density would be determined from trial mix/es of the proposed concrete that delivers the required strength and equ
